rows.notes: Top-left line used as a structural label. Handwriting of parish name is legible enough to read 'Tintinhull' and a following note that looks like 'Penny 2 pr' (could be 'Penny 2 yr' or '2 pr'); I chose '2 pr' to reflect the looped ending. No amounts present.
Second line under the top heading; reads as 'Beaton 2 pr' (handwriting ambiguous on final letters). No monetary amounts visible on this line.
Short single-word name that looks like 'Puckwin' or 'Puthwin' followed by '1 yr'. Writing is fairly faint and ambiguous; chosen reading is plausible but uncertain. No amounts on this row.

This line begins 'Little' followed by a heavily abbreviated/illegible name that I represent as 'Sc---/foloman' because letters are unclear. It ends '1 yr'. No amounts. Very uncertain due to ink flow and abbreviation.

Indented sub-item under 'Little ...' reads like 'T Gardener 3 yr' with an amount clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns as 11 | 3 | 6. Vertical ruling alignment used to locate columns; numbers in valid ranges.

Another indented sub-item; reads 'J Gardener 3 yr' (first initial ambiguous). Amount appears as '6 2' in the two rightmost columns; pence column is blank. Slight uncertainty which column holds the '2' but validated as shillings (0-19).
Name reads like 'Pember' (could be 'Pemben' etc.). Amount reads '25 | 10 | 1 1/2' — pence has a halfpenny mark interpreted as 0.5. Column alignment clear; values within valid ranges.
Parenthetical reads '(now Gorton)' faintly. Amount appears as '3 | 9 | 3'. Slight uncertainty on parentheses text but numeric columns align well.
Name reads 'Loude' (could be 'Lourie' or 'Louise'); amount aligned as '8 | 10 | 1'. Moderate handwriting ambiguity in name only; numbers clear.
Parish looks like 'Shurlington' (could be 'Shurlington' or similar). Amount reads '2 | 5 | 2' with clear alignment. Name legibility moderate.

Rightmost top-of-page total appears as 'Bromer 661 18 1 3/4'. I read the fraction as three-quarters (0.75). 'Bromer' reading uncertain (could be 'Browser' or 'Brumer') but numeric total is well-aligned and clear.
Centered heading clearly reads 'Money Rents Variable.' Very legible, used as structural header for following block.

rows.description: Petreon Qualifications, viz Ashfield Wants 3 years
rows.notes: Header-like long line beginning 'Petrean Qualifications, viz Ashfield ...' and an amount '5 8' to the right. Handwriting for parish string is faint but 'Ashfield' and '5 8' are legible; I treat it as an entry because it has an amount.

This small line under the qualifications block is quite faint; I read location as 'Dantwich Kent' and an amount '1' in the pounds column. Large uncertainty in both description and exact notation; amount alignment is faint but plausible.
Very faint line; I capture as 'Nantwich' followed by an unclear parenthetical and '1' in pounds column. Low confidence due to heavy fading and abbreviations.
Reads something like 'Ormsby Norfolk 1 yr' with an amount '1' (pounds). Slight uncertainty over punctuation and exact place name; amount column clearer than text.
Line reads 'Wootton Northamb[ton] 2 1/2' (the '5' appears in shillings column). Amount interpreted as 1 | 5 | (no pence). Handwriting for place and tenure partially legible; numbers align to columns.
Name uncertain (looks like 'Bodman' or 'Bodmon'). The amount reads '2 | 5' (pounds and shillings). Moderate reading uncertainty for name; numeric columns clearer.
A longer entry with a larger amount '32 | 19 | 5'. The description looks like 'Goldsmiths June' or similar; description is faint but amount is clear and used as alignment reference for this block.

Under the variable-rents block there is a small figure '8/7' or similar; placement suggests a small note or subtotal. Very faint and ambiguous whether this is pounds/shillings/pence. I record as a textual subtotal line with no parsed amounts because alignment is unclear.
Large, underlined total at center-right reads clearly as '784 0 5 1/2'. Column alignment is clear; interpreted pence fraction as halfpenny (0.5).
Clear centered Latin heading 'Recepta Anni praesentis' (Receipts of the present year). Very legible and used as a major section header.
Clear subheading 'Money Rents fixed.' marking the start of a new list of fixed rents.

First fixed-rents entry reads approximately 'Menthenoit Rectory 1 yr' (parish name uncertain). Amount to right reads '19 | 8 | 4' and alignment is good; description partly unclear but context (rectory) is evident.
Reads 'Kidlington Do - 1 yr' (Do indicating 'ditto' i.e. rectory/vicarage). Amount aligned as '9 | 14 | 10'. Letters are legible; numbers clear and within valid ranges.
Line begins 'Do (Capon & Brown)'; amount reads '1 | 6 | 8'. Parenthetical names are legible enough; numeric columns clear.

This row appears as 'Do Canal Company' with a right-side figure '18 2 1/4' — pence shows a farthing mark interpreted as 0.25. The pence whole digit is faint/possibly omitted; interpreted pence-whole as 0 and fraction 0.25 (but to conform to fields, I left amount_pence_whole blank and set fraction to .25). Slight ambiguity about whether there is a '0' in pence column.
Line reads 'Do Vicarage'; numeric columns show '3 | 17 | 8'. Reasonable legibility for numbers; description uses 'Do' abbreviation so context is clear.
Handwriting reads something like 'Brampton Regiment' or similar. Amount '1 | 13 | 4' is legible but name uncertain; moderate confidence.
Reads 'Hart Hall' with an amount that appears '1 | 13 | 4' (similar to previous line). Handwriting for the name is reasonably clear; numbers clear though slightly cramped.
Clearer entry 'St Martins Tenement' with amount '1 | 16 | 11'. All numeric values in valid range and alignment is consistent.
Parish name uncertain (reads 'Meecham' or 'Meckham' with a parenthetical 'Alma 83' that is unclear). Amount '1 | 9 | 6' fairly legible; description uncertain.
Reads 'Bushey Tenements' with amount '13 | 5 | 1'. Both description and numbers are reasonably clear, though the 'Bushey' spelling could vary historically.

Reads 'Waldron Do' (Do = same as previous category). A single '1' appears in the pounds column but the shillings/pence positions are faint or blank. I record pounds=1 and leave shillings/pence blank due to faintness.
Entry reads like 'St Nevison Cottage' or similar; an isolated '1' is visible in the pounds column only. Description uncertain and amount columns to right faint.
Reads 'L Wittenham Do' with a single '1' in the pounds column (shillings/pence not visible). 'L' likely abbreviation; reading of place has moderate uncertainty.
Clear centered heading 'Redeemed Land Tax'. Legible and marks a new sub-section.

First redeemed-tax line appears as 'Kidlington Rectory' with amount '10 | 16 | 2' (clear). There is an abbreviation before the amount that I could not fully decipher; numeric columns are clear and valid.
Reads 'Do Vicarage' with a clear amount '4 | 16 | 1'. 'Do' refers back to Kidlington. Numeric reading is clear; description uses abbreviation.
Entry reads 'L Wittenham Vicarage' with amount '4 | 4' (pence column blank). Slight uncertainty about the leading 'L' but numeric pounds and shillings align clearly.
Clear entry 'Yarnton Rectory' with amount '6 | 15 | 11'. Good alignment and legibility for both description and numbers.

Small bottom-right total shown as '8 4 8' — I interpret this as pounds 8, shillings 4, pence 8. Placement and writing match a concluding subtotal for the redeemed land-tax block; digits are somewhat faint but legible.
